findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is not rendered in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC state seal between the header text and 'NORTH CAROLINA Environmental Quality'. HTML omits this seal image.

findings.description: HTML page 2 merges content from multiple PDF pages into a single continuous flow, combining the letter page 1 content with the email chain and Colonial Pipeline letter content
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows a scanned copy of the same letter as page 1. HTML page 2 shows the continuation of the letter text plus header/footer information run together.

findings.description: The certified mail header information at top of page 2 in PDF is formatted differently in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Certified Mail / Mr. Threatt / Page Two' as underlined heading. HTML renders it inline.

findings.description: The DEQ logo at bottom of page 1 is not rendered in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows the DEQ logo with contact information. HTML only shows the text.

findings.description: The email chain structure with nested replies is flattened in the HTML version, losing the visual distinction between different email messages
findings.evidence: PDF shows clearly indented and visually separated email threads with headers. HTML presents them as a continuous flow of text with less visual separation.

findings.description: Social media icons (LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram) from Colonial Pipeline email signatures are missing in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows social media icon bar in email signatures. HTML omits these icons.

findings.description: Colonial Pipeline Company logo (CD logo) missing from email signature blocks in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows circular CD logo in email signatures. HTML omits this logo.

findings.description: Pages 5-8 contain a complex email chain with multiple nested levels that loses its hierarchical visual structure in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows clear visual nesting with indentation and separator lines between email messages. HTML flattens this structure.

findings.description: Bold italic formatting for emphasized text in Colonial Pipeline email is partially preserved but some italicized passages may not maintain their formatting
findings.evidence: PDF shows italic text for key phrases like 'The results of that initial analysis indicated...' HTML appears to render some of these in italic but inconsistently.

findings.description: NC State Seal image missing from Rep. Dennis Riddell's email signature
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC state seal image in Riddell's signature block. HTML omits this.

findings.description: Colonial Pipeline Company letterhead logo missing in HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF shows Colonial Pipeline CD logo and company name as letterhead. HTML only shows text.

findings.description: Underlined section headings in the Attachment 1 document may not be consistently rendered as underlined in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows underlined headings like 'Notice of Blasting Related Activities' and 'Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans'. HTML may use bold instead.

findings.description: The lettered and numbered list structure in Attachment 1 is generally preserved but spacing/indentation differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows carefully indented sub-items (a, b, c, etc.) under main items (A, B, C). HTML preserves the content but with different visual indentation.

findings.description: Site plan diagrams (Exhibits 1A and 1B) are present but may have reduced legibility in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows detailed engineering site plans with labels. HTML includes the images but the detail level and text on the plans may be harder to read.

findings.description: No h1 heading exists in the HTML document. The document lacks a proper title heading. The first heading encountered is an h3 ('Wehner, Judy'), which doesn't represent the document title.
findings.evidence:
The HTML uses

Wehner, Judy

as the first heading. There is no h1 for the overall document. The formal letter from NC DEQ and the Colonial Pipeline letter have no heading structure at all.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is broken and inconsistent. The document jumps to h3 without h1 or h2, and the major sections of the Colonial Pipeline Attachment 1 (e.g., 'BLASTING REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS', 'QUARRY OPERATION REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS') are not given heading tags at all.
findings.evidence: Sections I and II from Attachment 1 are rendered as inline bold text within paragraphs rather than as proper headings.

findings.description: The Blasting Plan information items (B.1.a through B.1.j) from Attachment 1 are rendered as a single block of text with lettered items separated by line breaks rather than as the structured sub-list format shown in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows items a-j as clearly indented sub-items under B.1. HTML renders them as a single numbered list item with items concatenated.

findings.description: Alt text for images is generic and sometimes inaccurate. Several images are described as 'Email correspondence page X' or 'Scanned Letter' which is acceptable but generic.
findings.evidence: Images use alt text like 'Scanned Letter from North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality', 'Email correspondence page 1', etc. These are generic but not fabricated.

findings.description: The email address 'report.spam@nc.gov' appears in the HTML CAUTION notices. This matches the PDF content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows 'report.spam@nc.gov' in the CAUTION banner, matching the HTML.

findings.description: The document includes both scanned page images AND the full text content of those pages. Every page is represented twice: once as an embedded image and once as extracted text. This creates significant duplication.
findings.evidence: For example, the DEQ letter on page 1 appears as AND as full text starting with 'ROY COOPER Governor...' The same pattern repeats for all 17 pages.
